channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Who could England face in last 32 after qualifying for next round?
item.description: England's place in the knockout stages of the World Cup is confirmed by other results before they face Panama in their final group game on Saturday.

item.title: Where does Scotland's World Cup campaign leave Clarke?
item.description: Did Steve Clarke get the best out of his Scotland squad at the 2026 World Cup as they face group-stage exit?

item.title: Gakpo asks for privacy after loss of unborn son
item.description: Noa van der Bij, the partner of Liverpool and Netherlands forward Cody Gakpo, announces the pair have lost their unborn son.

item.title: Who are the Panama players England need to look out for?
item.description: BBC Sport analyses the key players for Panama who will be aiming to cause a World Cup upset against England in their final Group L fixture.

item.title: 'Nothing is impossible' - Cape Verde make history to set up Argentina tie
item.description: How has a group of islands with just 525,000 inhabitants made it to the knockout rounds of the World Cup?

item.title: One night in Miami: The Scotsmen of the apocalypse
item.description: BBC Scotland's Scott Mullen paints the scene from Miami as Scotland's World Cup hopes fade.

item.title: Scotland's World Cup hopes at 0.07% - these are results they need
item.description: Defeat against Brazil left Scotland in a precarious position, but little has gone their way since either.

item.title: Chelsea in talks to sign Sunderland's Xhaka
item.description: Sunderland say experienced midfielder Granit Xhaka is not for sale amid reports Chelsea have entered talks over a move.

item.title: World Cup quiz: Name the team from England v Panama 2018
item.description: England face Panama for the first time since a 6-1 win at Russia 2018. Can you remember the starting XI?

item.title: Man Utd waiting on Ugarte injury after he leaves pitch on stretcher
item.description: Manchester United are waiting to discover the extent of Manuel Ugarte's injury after the midfielder was stretchered off during Uruguay's World Cup defeat by Spain.

item.title: 'No one could see it coming' - Tuchel defends selection after James injury
item.description: Thomas Tuchel defends his full-back selections after confirming Reece James will miss the match against Panama through injury.

item.title: VAR rules out late winner to deny Iran guaranteed last-32 spot
item.description: Shoja Khalilzadeh's goal is ruled out by VAR in injury-time denying Iran automatic qualification into the round of 32, meaning they will have to wait on other results to see if they go through as one of the best third-placed sides.

item.title: Belgium thrash New Zealand to win Group G
item.description: Belgium defeat New Zealand 5-1 to qualify for the World Cup round of 32 as winners of Group G on goal difference.

item.title: Egypt through but late drama denies Iran guaranteed last 32 spot
item.description: Iran have a goal ruled out for offside and hit the crossbar in the final moments of a thrilling 1-1 draw against Egypt in Seattle, leaving them hoping to secure qualification to the FIFA World Cup knockout stages by being one of the top eight third-placed teams.

item.title: Cape Verde's incredible World Cup journey continues after Saudi draw
item.description: Cape Verde deliver a valiant performance to earn their third draw of the group stage, a result that means they qualify for the last 32 in their World Cup debut while Saudi Arabia crash out.

item.title: Muslera mistake gifts Spain win as 10-man Uruguay crash out 'in disgrace'
item.description: Goalkeeper Fernando Muslera's mistake gifts Alex Baena the only goal of the game, as Spain defeat Uruguay to send Marcelo Bielsa's side out of the World Cup in the group stage.

item.title: 'The fairytale continues!' Emotional scenes as Cape Verde qualify for last 32
item.description: Jubilation and tears among Cape Verde fans and players as they confirm their 'miracle' qualification for the last 32 of the World Cup after earning a point against Saudi Arabia in Houston.

item.title: 'I leave nothing' - the end of 'toxic' Bielsa's Uruguay reign
item.description: With Uruguay eliminated from the 2026 World Cup, BBC Sport looks at what has gone wrong for them under colourful coach Marcelo Bielsa.

item.title: Messi to start final Argentina group match on bench
item.description: World Cup top scorer Lionel Messi will start Argentina's final group match against Jordan on the bench, manager Lionel Scaloni confirms.

item.title: We have been treated unfairly by USA - Iran
item.description: Iran coach Amir Ghalenoei says his nation have been treated unfairly by the United States during the World Cup

item.title: Why England should be wary of Panama's hybrid tactics
item.description: Panama's results do not paint a true picture of their performances in the World Cup so far - so what can England expect from them?

item.title: Are Celtic reacting quickly enough to last season's 'wake-up call'?
item.description: Have Celtic learned lessons from last season as Martin O'Neill sounds "wake-up call" warning?

item.title: Where to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

item.title: Catch up on the World Cup without any spoilers
item.description: It is a dilemma plenty of fans in the UK will have - and we are giving you the chance to follow the World Cup without spoilers.

item.title: From supermarket to World Cup - the forgotten England team
item.description: New Ferens Park feels a world away from the New York New Jersey Stadium, but it was on this artificial pitch in the north east of England where a future member of Thomas Tuchel's World Cup squad got what he called his "break".

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 20
item.description: Will you get today's World Cup player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Rest your best or go strong? Norway face questions as Haaland left out
item.description: Sensible choice or wrong decision? Norway leave Erling Haaland out as they make 10 changes against France - and lose heavily.

item.title: Chelsea close in on Lacroix - Saturday's gossip
item.description: Chelsea close in on Crystal Palace centre-back Maxence Lacroix, Newcastle United add Borussia Dortmund midfielder Felix Nmecha to their shortlist, while three Premier League clubs are chasing Uruguay left-back Maxi Araujo.

item.title: Spain's Pino may miss rest of World Cup
item.description: Spain winger Yeremy Pino may miss the rest of the World Cup with a suspected broken collarbone.

item.title: Brilliant Dembele hat-trick helps France see off Norway to top group
item.description: Ousmane Dembele's first-half hat-trick helps France soundly defeat a Norway side without Erling Haaland 4-1 to top Group I at the World Cup.

item.title: Senegal thrash 10-man Iraq to boost hopes of reaching last 32
item.description: Iliman Ndiaye and Ismaila Sarr produce a goal and an assist each as Senegal dismantle 10-man Iraq 5-0 at Toronto Stadium, boosting hopes of a World Cup last-32 spot.

item.title: 17 passes, all 11 players - Dembele scores 'magic' goal for France
item.description: Every member of France's starting XI gets involved as a 17-pass move is put away by Ousmane Dembele to seal his hat-trick in France's 4-1 win over Norway in their final FIFA World Cup Group I match at Boston Stadium.

item.title: Gueye scores 'venomous' double off the Senegal bench
item.description: Senegal midfielder Pape Gueye produced two moments of quality in front of goal soon after coming off the bench, scoring an impressive double against Iraq in their Group I battle to help fire his side into the last 32.

item.title: Dembele's 'magic' first-half hat-trick
item.description: France forward Ousmane Dembele stuns Norway with an incredible hat-trick in the opening 32 minutes of their Group I World Cup clash in Boston.

item.title: Hart explains how goalkeepers are struggling with World Cup ball
item.description: Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart answers your questions about the Trionda World Cup ball and how it might be affecting goalkeepers at the tournament.

item.title: Turkey end World Cup on high with last-gasp win over USA
item.description: Turkey come out on top in the last minute of an end-to-end 3-2 win over a second string USA side, in their final Group D match at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles.

item.title: Is a former England striker right about reasons for Scottish football's ills?
item.description: From undervalued broadcasting deals to unfulfilled potential, Ian Wright says he feels sorry for Scottish football fans - but is the former England striker right?

item.title: Why has Trump stayed away from the World Cup?
item.description: Why has Donald Trump stayed away from the World Cup? And will he appear before the final?

item.title: Ronaldo to score double? Sutton's World Cup 2026 score predictions - final group games
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the scores of the final set of group games at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: The 0-0 draw that highlighted flaw in World Cup format
item.description: Doubts rise over the World Cup's format as the first of the two group games that give the two teams a chance to progress with a draw finishes 0-0.

item.title: Arsenal poised to make Guimaraes offer after talks
item.description: Arsenal are poised to make an offer for Newcastle's Bruno Guimaraes after exploratory talks over potential moves for the Brazil midfielder and team-mate Sandro Tonali.

item.title: Why there's more to Spain than Lamine Yamal - Azpilicueta
item.description: Former Spain captain Cesar Azpilicueta explains why La Roja head coach Luis de la Fuente has the team and squad he needs for success in the next month.

item.title: Gunter's journey to mentor as Wales host U19 Euros
item.description: Chris Gunter won his first Wales senior cap as a 17-year-old in Wrexham - he returns to the Racecourse in charge of the next generation of Welsh talent.

item.title: Why are World Cup underdogs doing so well?
item.description: Are the surprise results achieved by lower-ranked teams in the World Cup a matter of luck - or clever planning and execution?

item.title: Has VAR become a lottery at the World Cup?
item.description: After a week of strange decisions many supporters have been left scratching their heads with VAR.

item.title: World Cup fans frustrated by 'confusing and expensive' tipping culture in US
item.description: International fans visiting the US for the World Cup have become frustrated by the culture of having to tip servers, telling the BBC that tipping fatigue has set in.

item.title: Start Rashford? Bring O'Reilly back? Pick your England XI to face Panama
item.description: Who should start for England against Panama in their final World Cup group game? It's time to put yourself in Thomas Tuchel's shoes.

item.title: How are the TV picks decided for the World Cup's round of 32?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the complexities behind television selections for the 2026 World Cup's round of 32 fixtures

item.title: How brands banned from the World Cup became the story
item.description: When we try to suppress something, we often make it more visible - and Fifa is living that in real time with its sponsors.

item.title: The Gakpo conundrum facing Liverpool
item.description: Cody Gakpo is thriving with the Netherlands but increased competition means his Liverpool future is uncertain.

item.title: Why it's time for Saka and Rashford - Shearer
item.description: Former England captain Alan Shearer explains why Thomas Tuchel needs to change some personnel as well as his team's mindset when they face Panama.

item.title: Tuchel's England are opposite to Southgate's - and built to beat top teams
item.description: BBC Sport's football tactics writer Umir Irfan explains why Thomas Tuchel's England system is built to prosper against the world's top nations.

item.title: World Cup's best matches, moments, goals & players so far - ranked
item.description: After two rounds of fixtures, BBC Sport ranks the best matches, moments, players and goals of the World Cup so far.

item.title: Quiz: How well do you know birthday boy Messi at World Cups?
item.description: Argentina great Lionel Messi turns 39 today and has been lighting up the World Cup this summer, but how well do you know his journey so far?

item.title: The beauty of sharing your child's first World Cup
item.description: There is nothing like the first World Cup you can enjoy with your kid - and the wide-eyed wonder through which they see it.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What is BBC's 3D Live Match Experience and how does it work?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the unique way of following matches during the 2026 World Cup via the BBC

item.title: Why Saudi Arabia and Iraq flags are not laid down at World Cup
item.description: The flags of Saudi Arabia and Iraq are not laid on the ground during World Cup pre-match ceremonies because they include religious wording.

item.title: Will three points guarantee a spot in World Cup knockouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at whether earning three points in the World Cup group stage will be enough to advance to the knockout rounds.

item.title: What are World Cup's red card suspension rules?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains Fifa's suspension rules on red and yellow cards during the World Cup.

item.title: How to watch highlights of World Cup matches
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how you can watch match highlights and full match replays during the 2026 Fifa World Cup

item.title: Why are footballers wearing pink boots at the World Cup?
item.description: The opening match of the 2026 World Cup was dominated by players wearing pink boots - but why is the colour so prevalent in football at the moment?

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Celtic not prepared to meet Hibs asking price for Chaiwa - gossip
item.description: Celtic continue chase for Hibs midfielder as Rangers consider a goalkeeper swap deal...

item.title: Why Vrancken marks fresh start at data-driven Hearts
item.description: As Belgian head coach Wouter Vranckin takes charge at Hearts, it marks a strengthening of the club's data-driven vision, writes Thomas Duncan.

item.title: 'Dream come true' - McCrorie returns to Rangers from Bristol City
item.description: Ross McCrorie is reuniting with manager Derek McInnes and returning to Rangers after Bristol City accept an undisclosed offer from the Ibrox club.

item.title: New Hearts boss Vrancken ready to challenge Old Firm
item.description: Hearts appoint former Genk and Sint-Truiden boss Wouter Vrancken as their new head coach a week after Derek McInnes left for Rangers.

item.title: Pattinson signs new deal with London City Lionesses
item.description: London City Lionesses has announced England defender Poppy Pattinson has extended her contract until summer 2029.

item.title: Double Ballon d'Or winner Putellas to join London City Lionesses
item.description: Two-time Ballon d'Or winner Alexia Putellas decides to move to London City Lionesses after leaving Barcelona.

item.title: Graham agrees permanent deal with Ipswich Women
item.description: Ipswich Town Women sign forward Kit Graham on a two-year contract following a successful loan from Tottenham last season.

item.title: Tottenham sign former Arsenal midfielder Pelova
item.description: Tottenham confirm the signing of Netherlands midfielder Victoria Pelova following her exit from Arsenal.

item.title: Godfrey signs new three-year deal with London City
item.description: England international Freya Godfrey has signed a new three-year contract with London City Lionesses.

item.title: Lazio to pay compensation in pregnancy dispute case
item.description: Lazio Women have been ordered to pay compensation to former midfielder Maja Gothberg because they unlawfully ended her employment due to pregnancy.

item.title: Brighton in advanced talks to sign Walti
item.description: Brighton are in advanced talks to sign Juventus and Switzerland midfielder Lia Walti.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
